summary: Re-ran `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched and applied the resulting duplicate-driven cleanups. The PR produced 5 new full matches and 3 partial improvements across item collision, game-mode time aggregation, fighter dynamics, stage animation, Kongo stage logic, and Link item behavior. The largest substantive change was restructuring `itcoll.c` collision routines with clearer early-continue loops and small static inline helpers while preserving matching-sensitive stack padding and control flow.
what_changed: Matched `gm_80163B9C` by changing the loop to accumulate `total_frames` across 0x19 records and return false immediately if `gmMainLib_8015D710(i)` fails, then compute hours/minutes/seconds/frame-percent once after the loop.
Matched `it_802703E8` in `itcoll.c` by converting nested `while`/`goto`-style control flow into early return, `for` loops, and `continue` filtering for owner/team/hitbox/hurtbox cases.

Improved `it_802706D0` in `itcoll.c` by extracting matching-sensitive static inline helpers `it_802706D0_sub`, `it_802706D0_sub2`, and `it_802706D0_sub3` for item hitbox prefiltering, hit-vs-hit collision handling, and hurtbox damage application.

Matched `ftCo_8009D81C` by renaming the local Kirby hat data pointer to `hat`, using `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, reading `bone_id` into a local `s32`, and writing `fp->dynamic_bone_sets[i].bone_id = FtPart_TopN` rather than mutating the source descriptor's `bone_id`.

Matched `grAnime_801C84A4` by replacing a duplicated local implementation of animation traversal/mask setup with a call to the existing helper `grAnime_801C8318`, retaining `PAD_STACK(8)` before checking `HSD_AObjGetFlags(aobj) & 0x04000000`.
Matched `grKongo_801D55D8` by adding `PAD_STACK(8)` and replacing `random_adder_f`/`random_adder` calls with `rand_range`, including casts for the float-backed values used in the first call.

Improved `itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim` by removing the saved `ok` boolean, calling `it_80272C6C(gobj)` directly in the conditional, using `PAD_STACK(12)`, and preserving a matching-sensitive dummy `if (ip != NULL)` block.

Improved `it_802A4BFC` in `itlinkhookshot.c` by adding explicit `Vec3` temporaries and unused stack pads, reducing `PAD_STACK(14 * 4)` to `PAD_STACK(7 * 4)`, and adding an alternate inline `it_802A2EE4_inline_alt_pad` for one motion-id case.
Applied mostly formatting and matching-sensitive expression layout changes in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, including call wrapping, brace placement, and long dat-attrs field access formatting.
Renamed a temporary in `gmMainLib_8015D984` from `qwe` to `temp_r31`, keeping the pointer-to-array-element assignment pattern.
smart_moves: Used the duplicate-discovery workflow (`table-typer dups`) as a targeted source of matching opportunities instead of hand-searching unrelated unmatched functions.
Reused an existing stage animation helper (`grAnime_801C8318`) rather than maintaining a duplicated traversal implementation in `grAnime_801C84A4`.

Split the very large `it_802706D0` item-collision routine into static inline pieces, allowing the source to become more readable while still giving the compiler the same inlined shape needed for matching.
Preserved matching-sensitive no-op structure with `PAD_STACK`, `UNUSED` local padding, and harmless dummy conditionals where necessary instead of over-cleaning the code.
Converted deeply nested collision filters into early returns/continues, which clarified the logical exclusions while also helping the compiler emit the desired control-flow shape.
Let decomp.dev quantify the result: +2020 matched bytes, +0.05% matched code, 5 new full matches, and 3 improvements.
decomp_lessons: After major matching progress, rerunning duplicate-finding tools can expose modest but real follow-up matches in unrelated systems.

Duplicate matching is often about structural equivalence: delegating to an already-matched helper or extracting a repeated inline block can be more effective than local instruction-by-instruction tweaking.

For collision code, branch inversion with early `continue` statements can remove gotos while retaining equivalent filtering order, but each side effect such as damage accumulation, toucher assignment, sound effect choice, and break behavior must remain grounded in the original flow.

Stack-shape artifacts are part of the match: `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, expression-form padding such as `2 * 4`, and unused local arrays can be necessary even when they look semantically redundant.
Local variable naming can be matching-driven: names such as `temp_r31` document expected register allocation, while clearer names such as `hat` are safe when they do not disturb codegen.
Replacing a duplicated implementation with an existing helper can improve both maintainability and match rate, but only when the helper's side effects and null behavior are demonstrably equivalent.
naming_conventions: Use `temp_r31`-style names when the local is clearly register-allocation/matching driven, as in `gmMainLib_8015D984`.
Prefer descriptive locals when they clarify known data, as in `KirbyHatStruct* hat` replacing `data` in `ftCo_8009D81C`.

Extracted static inline helpers in unmatched/generated-code areas may keep address-like prefixes such as `it_802706D0_sub`, `it_802706D0_sub2`, and `it_802706D0_sub3` when no semantic names are evidenced.
Existing project-style macros and names were preserved: `GET_ITEM`, `GET_FIGHTER`, `PAD_STACK`, `UNUSED`, `HitCapsule`, `HurtCapsule`, `Item_GObj`, `Fighter_GObj`.
No reviewer-provided naming guidance was present in the slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Re-ran `table-typer dups` to locate duplicate-shaped unmatched functions.
Used helper delegation to collapse `grAnime_801C84A4` into `grAnime_801C8318` plus `PAD_STACK(8)` and a flag check.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, and `PAD_STACK(7 * 4)` to tune frame layout.
Added `UNUSED` stack padding locals and separate `Vec3 pos0`, `pos1`, `pos2` temporaries in `it_802A4BFC` to affect stack layout and lifetime.
Introduced an alternate inline helper `it_802A2EE4_inline_alt_pad` with a dummy `UNUSED f32 pad` and local matrix setup for one Link hookshot case.
Used branch inversion and early `continue`/`return` to match item collision control flow while avoiding explicit gotos.
Changed random helper calls in `grKongo_801D55D8` from `random_adder_f`/`random_adder` to `rand_range` with argument order/casts matching the target.
Kept apparently redundant or non-semantic constructs such as `if (ip != NULL) {}` in `itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim` because they affected the match.
review_feedback: PR body credits Jelle with suggesting a re-run of `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 69.90%, +0.05%, +2020 bytes.
decomp.dev reported 5 new full matches: `gm_80163B9C`, `it_802703E8`, `ftCo_8009D81C`, `grAnime_801C84A4`, and `grKongo_801D55D8`.
No human review comments or requested changes were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Search for other functions still duplicating `grAnime_801C8318` logic.
Run or inspect relaxed `table-typer dups` results as the PR author planned.
Look for other item collision routines that can reuse the `it_802706D0` helper-extraction pattern.
Audit remaining near-matches that may only need stack padding or dummy UNUSED locals.
Compare functions using `random_adder` or `random_adder_f` against `rand_range` call shapes for possible duplicate matches.
